evaluations.name: Morning and Evening Occupancy Peaks
evaluations.recommendation: Pre-heat hot water 30 min before peak to avoid instant-draw spikes.

evaluations.name: High Night-Time Base Load
evaluations.message: Building is consuming 0.944% of daytime energy overnight.
evaluations.recommendation: Investigate equipment left running overnight. Schedule HVAC setback.

evaluations.name: Demand Spike Detection
evaluations.message: Peak demand is 1.9x the average — opportunity to flatten load curve.
evaluations.recommendation: Implement demand response: stagger HVAC start, shift non-critical loads.

evaluations.name: Hot Water / Steam Proportion
evaluations.recommendation: Install heat pump water heaters — 3x more efficient than electric.

evaluations.name: Weather Sensitivity Check
evaluations.message: Energy use is 46% correlated with outdoor temperature.
evaluations.recommendation: Optimize HVAC based on temperature forecast to reduce reactive energy spikes.

evaluations.name: Seasonal Energy Pattern
evaluations.recommendation: Seasonal HVAC tuning can yield 10-20% energy savings.

evaluations.name: Energy Waste During Low Occupancy
evaluations.message: Weekend occupancy drop not reflected in energy savings — review floor shutdowns.
evaluations.recommendation: Smart floor management — power down unoccupied floors automatically.
